title: tragacantha bustillosii
intro: Tragacantha bustillosii, also known as Mexican milk thistle or wild artichoke, is a perennial plant native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its thistle-like flowers and edible stems. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a natural diuretic and to support liver health, making it a popular remedy for liver and gallbladder issues. The health benefits of Tragacantha bustillosii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used to support overall health and well-being.
